avoid: Do not trust readable TARGET or VAL strings because the file contains decoys.
Do not hardcode the XOR key, payload offset, or payload length.
Do not skip checksum validation before accepting the decoded payload.
Do not include or replay a prior decoder implementation as source.
learned_facts: Inspect the binary file as indexed bytes rather than treating it as plain text.
Require the ZB2 magic marker before interpreting the compact header.
Read the dynamic XOR key from the key byte in the header.
Read the payload offset and payload length from their header bytes.
Slice only the encoded payload described by the header.
XOR payload bytes with the dynamic key and reverse the payload in the learned order.
Validate the decoded payload checksum against the trailing checksum byte.
Extract the real TARGET value only from the validated decoded payload.

allowed: Inspect and decode an owned local benchmark fixture.
Create a fresh local decoder under sandbox file policy.
Run the deterministic local verifier against the current fixture.
forbidden: Reusing the procedure to access data without authorization.
Accepting visible decoy text as verified output.
Pasting historical decoder source into guidance.

evidence: The real MacGyver filesystem benchmark requires the decoded TARGET to match the hidden fixture value.
The real MacGyver A/B benchmark compares no-memory and Howdex-guided student runs.
limitations: The procedure applies only to the documented ZB2 benchmark format.
It is not a general binary-analysis or security authorization.

title: Recover the validated TARGET from a ZB2 binary fixture

verification.expected_signal: The verifier reports SUCCESS and the decoder output contains the true hidden TARGET value.
